entries.statement: Knowledge of SQL for data querying, including joins, aggregations, window functions, subqueries, and query optimization fundamentals.

Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard library capabilities, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).

Knowledge of relational database management system concepts, including normalization theory, indexing strategies, transaction isolation levels, query execution plans, and performance optimization principles.

Knowledge of NoSQL database categories and their appropriate use cases, including document stores, key-value stores, wide-column stores, graph databases, and vector databases, with understanding of when each category is preferred over relational alternatives.

Knowledge of cloud platform services for data and AI workloads across major providers, including compute, storage, managed database, analytics, and machine learning services, and the trade-offs between cloud-native and cloud-agnostic approaches.

Knowledge of distributed computing concepts relevant to data processing at scale, including data partitioning strategies, parallel execution models, fault tolerance mechanisms, data locality optimization, and the CAP theorem.

Knowledge of data serialization formats and their trade-offs for different workloads, including JSON, Apache Avro, Apache Parquet, and Protocol Buffers, with understanding of schema evolution, compression characteristics, and query performance implications.

Knowledge of software version control concepts and Git workflows, including branching strategies, merge and rebase operations, conflict resolution, pull request processes, and collaborative development practices.

Knowledge of CI/CD pipeline concepts for data and AI projects, including continuous integration principles, automated testing integration, artifact management, deployment automation patterns, and environment promotion strategies.

Knowledge of containerization and orchestration concepts for data workloads, including Docker container principles, image layering and optimization, Kubernetes pod and service architecture, and resource management for data-intensive containers.

Knowledge of API design patterns and protocols for data services, including REST architectural constraints, GraphQL query patterns, gRPC service definitions, authentication and authorization mechanisms, and API versioning strategies.

Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.

Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.

Knowledge of computing fundamentals relevant to data and AI work, including computer architecture concepts (processors, memory hierarchy, storage systems), operating system principles, hardware-software interaction, and how infrastructure constraints affect data processing performance and system design decisions.
entries.strm_gap_source: STRM-001-ONET: O*NET 2.C.3.a (Computers and Electronics) — STS 0.361 to AI-K-016
